pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates it and export_result.sh checks files within it.
setup_task.sh generates galaxy_survey_plan.txt with RA/Dec J2000 coordinates that mathematically convert exactly to these decimal values, which verifier.py also strictly compares against.
galaxy_survey_plan.txt explicitly requests R, V, B filter slots, and verifier.py strictly grades for these filters.
galaxy_survey_plan.txt specifies '>=2' frames per filter, and verifier.py awards maximum points when the frame count per filter is 2 or higher.
verifier.py checks if the minimum separation is <= coord_tol_deg (which it loads from metadata falling back to 2.0).
verifier.py strictly evaluates if the generated false-color composite PNG size is > 50000 bytes.
setup_task.sh instructs the agent to create this exact file path, and export_result.sh explicitly checks for its existence and modification time.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates entirely using synthetically generated FITS frames from the KStars INDI CCD simulator. It requires collecting RGB images (>= 2 frames per R, V, B filter) for 4 specific targets (M87, M104, M51, NGC4449) based on real J2000 coordinates provided in the generated survey plan text. The expected output requires the images to be saved in '/home/ga/Images/galaxy_survey', false-color composites > 50000 bytes to be created, and a summary catalog output to '/home/ga/Documents/galaxy_survey_catalog.txt'.
